title: Merge ladder RUNG 2: real package overlap gets a scoped merge-review of the conflicting hunks only — full re-audit (rung 3) becomes the rare entangled case
summary: OPERATOR-PRIORITIZED (ladder rung 3 of 4; rung 4 = existing full re-audit, no work needed). When rungs 0-1 do not apply (overlapping package footprints), dispatch a SCOPED merge-review instead of a full re-audit: a fast/balanced-tier reviewer receives ONLY the intersecting hunks, both change summaries, and the interaction question (does the composition break either change's intent?). Verdict compatible => composition-verdict{method:scoped-review} + native gates; verdict entangled => rung 3 full re-audit (today's behavior). Any LLM-assisted conflict RESOLUTION produces a new tree that must re-enter rung 0 verification (patch-id vs resolved diff; LLM merges are suggestion-grade per MergeBERT-lineage evidence). Deterministic structural merge (mergiraf-class, tree-sitter) MAY be used as a pre-step to shrink the conflict set, but its output is verified, never trusted.
acceptance: RED: TestScopedReview_SeesOnlyIntersectingHunks (prompt contains conflict regions, not full diffs)
